skill.name: azure-ai-vision-imageanalysis-java
skill.description: Build image analysis applications with Azure AI Vision SDK for Java. Use when implementing image captioning, OCR text extraction, object detection, tagging, or smart cropping.
skill.summary: Build image analysis applications with Azure AI Vision SDK for Java. Use when implementing image cap...
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This is a legitimate Azure AI Vision SDK documentation skill for Java. Static analysis flagged many patterns but all are false positives: (1) External commands detected at lines 15-268 are Maven XML and Java code examples, not shell commands. (2) Network URLs at lines 102, 269, 281 are example/demo URLs and documentation links. (3) Environment variable access at lines 32-33 uses System.getenv() for secure credential management - this is the recommended secure practice. (4) High/blocker findings for 'weak cryptographic algorithm' are YAML metadata and table formatting, not cryptographic code. The skill uses proper security practices by reading Azure credentials from environment variables rather than hardcoding them.

low_findings.title: External Commands Detection - False Positive
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 38 instances of 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' at various lines (15-268). Upon manual review, these are Maven XML dependency definitions and Java code examples - NOT shell commands. The analyzer incorrectly identified XML tags like <groupId> and <artifactId> as shell backticks.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs - False Positive
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 3 hardcoded URLs. These are all legitimate: (1) Line 102: example.com demo URL in sample code, (2) Line 269: Azure endpoint template for environment configuration, (3) Line 281: Microsoft documentation link. No credential exfiltration risk.

low_findings.title: Environment Variable Access - Legitimate Secure Practice
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged System.getenv() calls at lines 32-33. This is the SECURE recommended practice for handling Azure credentials - reading from environment variables rather than hardcoding them in source code. This is NOT a security vulnerability.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm - False Positive
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 'weak cryptographic algorithm' at lines 3, 65, 67, 281, 294. These are: Line 3 (YAML: 'risk: unknown'), lines 65-67 (Markdown table formatting), line 281 (Azure documentation URL), line 294 (skill description). No cryptographic code present.

dangerous_patterns.title: Critical Heuristic: Code Execution + Network + Credential Access - FALSE POSITIVE
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer flagged 'DANGEROUS COMBINATION: Code execution + Network + Credential access' as critical. This is standard Azure SDK usage pattern, NOT a security threat. The skill demonstrates proper use of Azure AI Vision SDK where: (1) Code executes SDK methods for image analysis, (2) Network calls are made to Azure Cognitive Services API, (3) Credentials are read from environment variables (secure practice). This combination is expected behavior for any Azure SDK integration and does not indicate malicious activity.

content.user_title: Build image analysis apps with Azure AI Vision Java SDK
content.value_statement: Need to add image captioning, OCR text extraction, object detection, or smart cropping to your Java application. This skill provides ready-to-use code patterns for integrating Azure AI Vision SDK with proper credential management and error handling.
actual_capabilities: Generate human-readable image captions with confidence scores
Extract text from images using OCR with bounding box coordinates
Detect objects in images with bounding boxes and confidence levels
Tag images with content labels for categorization
Detect people in images with location coordinates
Create smart crop thumbnails with aspect ratio optimization
limitations: Requires Azure AI Vision subscription and valid API credentials
Image size limited to 20MB and dimensions between 50x50 and 16000x16000 pixels
Caption and Dense Captions features require GPU-supported Azure regions
Supported image formats: JPEG, PNG, GIF, BMP, WEBP, ICO, TIFF, MPO

use_cases.title: Automate document digitization
use_cases.description: Extract text from scanned documents, receipts, and invoices to create searchable digital archives. Use OCR to convert paper documents into editable text.
use_cases.target_user: Enterprise developers building document management systems

use_cases.title: Enhance e-commerce product catalogs
use_cases.description: Automatically generate product image captions and detect objects for better search indexing. Create consistent smart-cropped thumbnails for uniform catalog display.

use_cases.title: Build accessibility features
use_cases.description: Generate image descriptions for visually impaired users. Provide automatic alt text for images in content management systems.

prompt_templates.title: Basic image caption
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Azure AI Vision Java SDK to generate a caption for the image at local file path /path/to/image.jpg. Show how to set up the client with environment variables and print the caption text with confidence score.

prompt_templates.title: Extract text from document
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to use the Azure AI Vision SDK to perform OCR on a scanned document image. Include code to iterate through detected text blocks, lines, and words with their bounding polygons.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-feature analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Write Java code that analyzes an image using multiple visual features: caption, tags, objects, and read (OCR). Show how to configure the analysis options for gender-neutral captions and specify language.

prompt_templates.title: Async image processing
prompt_templates.prompt: Demonstrate how to use the async client in Azure AI Vision Java SDK to analyze images. Show the subscription pattern for handling async results and errors.

output_examples.output: The Azure AI Vision SDK generates a caption like: 'A group of people sitting at a conference table discussing a presentation' with a confidence score of 0.92. The code shows how to extract both the text and confidence value.
OCR results include structured text blocks with line and word-level details. Each word shows its text, confidence score (e.g., 0.98), and bounding polygon coordinates for precise positioning.

Object detection returns an array of detected objects. Each object includes tags with names (e.g., 'person', 'laptop') and confidence scores, plus bounding box coordinates (x, y, width, height) for drawing detection boxes.

best_practices: Always store Azure credentials in environment variables, never hardcode API keys in source code
Use async clients for high-volume image processing to improve throughput
Handle HttpResponseException for robust error handling and retry logic
anti_patterns: Do not hardcode API keys or endpoint URLs in your Java source files
Do not ignore error handling - always catch and log Azure service exceptions
Do not process images larger than 20MB - resize before analysis to avoid failures

faq.question: How do I authenticate with Azure AI Vision?
faq.answer: You can authenticate using an API key with KeyCredential or use DefaultAzureCredential for managed identity support. Store your VISION_ENDPOINT and VISION_KEY in environment variables.

faq.question: What image formats are supported?
faq.answer: Azure AI Vision supports JPEG, PNG, GIF, BMP, WEBP, ICO, TIFF, and MPO formats. Image size must be under 20MB with dimensions between 50x50 and 16000x16000 pixels.

faq.question: Can I analyze images from a URL?
faq.answer: Yes, use the analyzeFromUrl() method to analyze images accessible via HTTP URLs. The SDK also supports analyzing local files using BinaryData.fromFile().

faq.question: How accurate is the caption feature?
faq.answer: Caption confidence scores typically range from 0.0 to 1.0. Higher scores indicate more reliable captions. Dense Captions provides multiple descriptions for different image regions.

faq.question: What languages are supported for OCR?
faq.answer: Azure AI Vision OCR supports over 90 languages. Specify the language in ImageAnalysisOptions using setLanguage() for best results.

faq.question: Does this work with on-premise Azure Stack?
faq.answer: Yes, Azure AI Vision SDK can connect to Azure Stack or other Azure Cognitive Services instances by setting the appropriate endpoint URL to your local service.
